questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.text: To sell premium Notion subscriptions
options.explanation: The article does not mention selling subscriptions; it focuses on learning.

options.text: To provide structured learning paths, lessons, and badges for mastering Notion
options.explanation: Correct – the article explains that Notion Academy offers curated paths, lessons, and badges.

options.text: To host a marketplace for third‑party Notion templates
options.explanation: While templates are mentioned, the marketplace is a separate feature, not the core purpose of the Academy.

options.text: To replace the Notion Help Center entirely
options.explanation: The Help Center is still referenced for quick answers; the Academy supplements it.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Notion Certified Admins is true?

options.text: Certification is only available for enterprise‑level plans
options.explanation: The article says certification is available for any plan type.

options.text: Certified Admins are recognized for implementing security best practices at scale
options.explanation: Correct – the article highlights security, scaling, and recognition.

options.text: The certification requires completing all beginner‑level lessons
options.explanation: Certification is not tied to a specific lesson level; it focuses on admin competencies.

options.text: Certification replaces the need for a Notion workspace
options.explanation: Certification is a credential, not a replacement for a workspace.

questions.question: If a learner wants to focus on building automated workflows in Notion, which path should they choose?

options.text: Essentials Path
options.explanation: Essentials Path covers basic skills, not advanced workflow automation.

options.text: Workflows Path
options.explanation: Correct – the Workflows Path is described as creating connected systems and using AI.

options.text: Advanced Path
options.explanation: Advanced Path includes automation but also broader architecture; Workflows Path is more focused.

options.text: Custom Agents Path
options.explanation: Custom Agents are a feature, not a named learning path in the article.

questions.question: Which lesson would best help a beginner understand how to organize knowledge using Notion’s built‑in tools?

options.text: Building basics
options.explanation: This beginner lesson covers navigation, blocks, custom databases, and wikis for knowledge management.

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: This lesson is intermediate and focuses on forms, charts, and automations.

options.text: Templates & marketplace
options.explanation: This lesson is intermediate and centers on creating and selling templates.

options.text: Workspace design
options.explanation: Workspace design is an advanced lesson about best practices, not a beginner overview.

questions.question: What distinguishes a ‘Badged Path’ from a regular learning path in Notion Academy?

options.text: Badged Paths automatically grant a Notion Certified Admin badge upon completion
options.explanation: Certification requires a separate process; Badged Paths only award learning badges.

options.text: Badged Paths are structured series of lessons that culminate in a badge to showcase the skill
options.explanation: Correct – the article describes Badged Paths as collections of lessons that earn badges.

options.text: Badged Paths are exclusive to enterprise customers
options.explanation: The article does not limit Badged Paths to any plan type.

options.text: Badged Paths require live webinar attendance for each lesson
options.explanation: Live webinars are optional and not a requirement for Badged Paths.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why the article includes a ‘Custom Agents’ section, even though the focus is on database views?

options.text: Custom Agents are a type of database view that automatically filters records
options.explanation: Custom Agents are automation tools, not a database view.

options.text: The section illustrates how automations can complement database views to reduce busywork
options.explanation: Correct – the article highlights that Custom Agents automate repeat work, which can be combined with database views for efficient workflows.

options.text: Custom Agents replace the need for any database view in Notion
options.explanation: Agents augment, not replace, database views.

options.text: The inclusion is an editorial error unrelated to the lesson’s content
options.explanation: While the article’s focus is broader, the inclusion is intentional to show related capabilities.

questions.question: According to the article, what is the recommended way for a learner to get quick answers to detailed Notion questions?

options.text: Attend a live webinar
options.explanation: Webinars are for deeper skill development, not quick Q&A.

options.text: Visit the Help Center
options.explanation: The article explicitly mentions the Help Center for quick answers.

options.text: Join the Notion community forum
options.explanation: Community is for sharing stories and inspiration, not the primary quick‑answer resource.

options.text: Read the ‘Templates & marketplace’ lesson
options.explanation: That lesson focuses on templates, not general troubleshooting.
